meaning: n.似非而是的论点,自相矛盾的话
examples: The story contains many levels of paradox.
He was a paradox—a loner who loved to chat to strangers.
It is a curious paradox that professional comedians often have unhappy personal lives.
The United States, the wealthiest nation on earth, presents the flip side of this paradox.
As we drive back through the valley to our motel, the nothing-something paradox prods at me.
